description: MCP server args contain a hardcoded absolute Windows path c:\Users\LeoDg\.claude\... which breaks the MCP server for every user that is not the developer; all non-developer installs silently fail to start the MCP server
suggested_fix: Compute the path at install time and write it into .mcp.json during install.sh execution, or use a wrapper script with a relative path

description: Step 2 of the installer runs `curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh` without checksum verification; a compromised CDN or MitM response executes arbitrary code with user privileges at install time
suggested_fix: Download the script to a temp file, verify its SHA256 against the published checksum at https://github.com/astral-sh/uv/releases, then execute; or pin to a specific UV version using the documented versioned install URL

description: postinstall script runs `npm run build` (tsc + copy-assets) automatically on every npm install; enables arbitrary code execution at install time if a dependency is compromised or a malicious version is published
suggested_fix: Remove the postinstall hook; require users to run `npm run build` explicitly after cloning, or document it in the README install steps

description: cleanup_orphan_processes() calls subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True) on a hardcoded cmd string; while not currently user-influenced, shell=True widens the injection surface and is unnecessary when cmd can be expressed as a list
suggested_fix: Replace shell=True with shell=False and express each command as a list, e.g. ['taskkill', '/F', '/IM', 'python.exe', '/FI', 'MEMUSAGE gt 100000']

description: Agent contains explicit instructions for bypassing MetaTrader EA copy protections: account number validation bypass, HWID fingerprinting removal, trial/expiry check removal, broker restriction bypass, and demo-mode removal; references EX4-TO-MQL, IDA Pro, Ghidra, x64dbg, OllyDbg for protection circumvention; distributing these instructions in a public plugin enables DMCA-violating usage at scale
suggested_fix: Remove the protection bypass sections; scope the agent to legitimate reverse engineering use cases (debugging own code, compatibility analysis) with explicit authorization disclaimers; or gate behind an opt-in install flag

description: Agent references Cobalt Strike, Sliver C2, and explicit EDR/AV bypass techniques for red-team operations; no authorization gate exists between the agent and any end user; public plugin distribution means any user who installs it gets an AI configured to provide detailed offensive toolchain guidance
suggested_fix: Add an explicit authorization disclaimer requiring proof of engagement scope before agent activates; or move to a separate opt-in package not included in the default install; replace Cobalt Strike/Sliver references with generic red-team concepts

description: CLAUDE.md instructs Claude to run taskkill /F on python.exe, node.exe, bash.exe, git.exe, grep.exe, cmd.exe, and powershell.exe at the end of every task; this terminates all matching processes system-wide regardless of whether they belong to the plugin or to unrelated user work; the NUL KILLER V2.0 block uses ctypes.windll.kernel32.DeleteFileW on user home directories
suggested_fix: Scope cleanup to processes explicitly spawned by the plugin session using the ProcessManager job-object pattern already implemented in lib/process_manager.py; remove the blanket taskkill instructions from CLAUDE.md

description: args array contains c:\Users\LeoDg\.claude\plugins\orchestrator-plugin\mcp_server\server.py which does not exist on any machine except the developer's; MCP server fails to start on all user installs; this is a ship-blocking defect
suggested_fix: Replace with a dynamic path computed by install.sh: write the correct absolute path into .mcp.json during installation using the actual ORCHESTRATOR_DIR value

description: References experts/iam_expert.md and experts/api_expert.md; neither file exists; iam_expert was merged into experts/security_unified_expert.md and api_expert was merged into experts/integration_expert.md in a prior refactor
suggested_fix: Replace experts/iam_expert.md with experts/security_unified_expert.md and experts/api_expert.md with experts/integration_expert.md

description: Contains five broken expert file references: experts/api_expert.md, experts/mql5_expert.md, experts/pyqt5_expert.md, experts/python_expert.md, experts/iam_expert.md; all were renamed in V6+ refactors
suggested_fix: Update to: experts/integration_expert.md, experts/mql_expert.md, experts/gui-super-expert.md, experts/languages_expert.md, experts/security_unified_expert.md respectively

description: Header section reads '44 Totali' but footer reads '43 agents total (6 L0 + 22 L1 + 15 L2)'; the math 6+22+15=43 is correct; header is off by one
suggested_fix: Change '44 Totali' to '43 Totali' in the header table caption

description: Module docstring says 'Total Agents: 36 (6 Core + 15 L1 Expert + 15 L2 Sub-Agent)' and the orchestrator_config resource returns total_agents:36; actual deployed count is 43; the 7 experts added after V6 (reverse_engineering, offensive_security, mql_decompilation, browser_automation, mcp_integration, notification, payment_integration) are not in KEYWORD_TO_EXPERT_MAPPING and are unreachable via MCP routing
suggested_fix: Add the 7 missing experts to KEYWORD_TO_EXPERT_MAPPING and update total_agents to 43 in the resource response and module docstring

description: INTEGRATION_REPORT.md documents 11 expert agents (V5.1 integration) while the current system has 22 L1 experts; the architecture diagram and integration metrics are approximately 50% behind reality
suggested_fix: Regenerate the integration report to reflect V12.0 state with 22 L1 experts and 15 L2 sub-agents; update all counts, architecture diagrams, and routing tables in the doc

description: CLAUDE.md contains two conflicting agent counts in the same file: header says 44, footer says 43; creates confusion about the canonical agent count
suggested_fix: Align both to 43 and establish a single canonical count updated whenever agents are added or removed

description: Frontmatter declares name and description but omits model; routing tables indicate this agent should use haiku but that is expressed only in inline documentation, not in machine-readable frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add `model: haiku` to the YAML frontmatter block

description: Frontmatter declares name and description but omits model; routing tables indicate sonnet but that is not in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add `model: sonnet` to the YAML frontmatter block

description: Frontmatter declares name and description but omits model; routing tables indicate sonnet but that is not in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add `model: sonnet` to the YAML frontmatter block

description: Frontmatter declares name and description but omits model; routing tables indicate haiku but that is not in frontmatter
suggested_fix: Add `model: haiku` to the YAML frontmatter block
